businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, and energy
businessSummary.description: Tesla is transitioning from EV manufacturing to an integrated AI/robotics/energy platform. FY2025 revenue $94.8B (-2.9% YoY), net income $3.79B (-46.5% YoY). Friday close $391.20 (-0.96%). Reuters: 'delivery slide may stretch to third year.' European registrations +10% YoY rebound, but Motley Fool calls it 'far from a strong rebound.' VP of Finance Sendil Palani departed after 17 years (executive exodus growing). GTC 2026 (3/16-19) opens Monday — Jensen Huang keynote to spotlight autonomous driving and agentic AI infrastructure. FSD EU approval pushed to 3/20. Market cap $1.47T, P/E ~387x.
howTheyMakeMoney: Vehicle sales: ~73% of revenue ($69.5B). Model 3/Y core, Model S/X discontinuation in Q2 2026
Energy business: ~14% of revenue ($12.8B, +27% YoY). Megapack/Powerwall growth
FSD/Software: License revenue ~$3B. Accelerating subscription conversion

keyMetrics.name: P/E Ratio
keyMetrics.interpretation: Historically overvalued. Extremely expensive relative to current earnings.

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Still premium valuation even factoring FY2026 estimates.

keyMetrics.name: P/S Ratio
keyMetrics.interpretation: Overvalued vs. revenue. Reflects AI/robotics story premium.

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Revenue decline. Concerns about 3rd consecutive year of delivery drops.

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Margin crushed by price cuts. Down from 17% in FY2022 to 4.6%.

keyMetrics.name: FCF
keyMetrics.interpretation: Positive cash flow maintained. Solid against $8.5B capex.

keyMetrics.name: Debt/Equity
keyMetrics.interpretation: Healthy balance sheet. Net cash position of $29.3B.

keyMetrics.name: ROE
keyMetrics.interpretation: Return on equity sharply declined. Profitability recovery is key.

growthDrivers.title: GTC 2026 Opens Monday — Autonomous Driving & Agentic AI Spotlight
growthDrivers.description: NVIDIA GTC (3/16-19) with Jensen Huang unveiling agentic AI CPU pivot. Indirect catalyst for Tesla FSD/robotaxi ecosystem. CNBC: 'CPUs take center stage at GTC.'

growthDrivers.title: European Registrations +10% YoY — First Rebound After 13 Consecutive Months of Decline
growthDrivers.description: Motley Fool (3/14): European EV registrations up 10% YoY, breaking 13-month decline streak. However, 'far from a strong rebound' — subsidy cuts persist in Germany/France.

growthDrivers.title: FSD EU Approval Expected 3/20 — Regulatory Milestone Imminent
growthDrivers.description: FSD Netherlands approval pushed to 3/20. If approved, EU-wide expansion possible. NHTSA data submission (3/9) completed.

growthDrivers.title: China February EV Sales +91% YoY — Strong Local Momentum
growthDrivers.description: FX Leaders (3/11): Strong China sales growth + margin improvement drove 3% stock rebound. Notable vs. BYD slowdown.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: US EV market 50%+ share leader. Software/manufacturing/energy/FSD 8.2B-mile vertical integration is the core moat. However, overtaken globally by BYD.

moats.description: 8.2 billion FSD miles driven. Overwhelming training data advantage vs. competitors.
In-house batteries (4680), custom chips (HW4), software (FSD), charging infrastructure (Supercharger).
NACS is the North American EV charging standard. GM, Ford, Rivian depend on Tesla Superchargers.

competitors.name: Lucid (LCID)
competitors.detail: Washington state direct sales bill passed. Premium EV competition accelerating

competitors.name: Rivian (RIVN)
competitors.detail: Washington state grants equal direct sales rights as Tesla. R2 mass market entry

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Autonomous taxi service expanding. L4 autonomy leader

recentNews.headline: European EV registrations +10% YoY, first rebound after 13 consecutive months of decline
recentNews.significance: Bottom signal for sales, but Motley Fool warns 'far from a strong rebound.' Subsidy cuts continue.

recentNews.headline: Reuters: Tesla delivery slide may stretch to third year, cash burn looms
recentNews.significance: Investors and analysts cutting delivery estimates. Revenue/profit pressure intensifying.

recentNews.headline: Strong China sales + Digital Optimus focus drives TSLA +3%
recentNews.significance: China sales momentum and margin improvement provide short-term stock support.

recentNews.headline: VP of Finance Sendil Palani departs after 17 years — executive exodus grows
recentNews.significance: Core executive departure from Tesla's earliest days. Management stability concerns.

recentNews.headline: Washington state passes direct sales bill — Rivian & Lucid gain Tesla-equal rights
recentNews.significance: Tesla's direct sales exclusivity eroding. Long-term market share pressure from competitors.

analystOpinions.notableComment: 30 analysts consensus Hold, avg target $396.15 (+1.3%). MarketBeat $406.84. TipRanks $399.25. Extremely divided — BofA $460 Buy vs. bear case $25.

risks.title: Potential third consecutive year of delivery decline
risks.description: Reuters reports investors and analysts forecasting another delivery decline in 2026. Revenue and profit pressure.

risks.title: Extreme valuation premium
risks.description: P/E 387x, Forward P/E 189x, PEG 4.02. Difficult to justify on current earnings.

risks.title: Executive exodus accelerating
risks.description: VP Finance departed after 17 years. Management stability risk increasing.

risks.title: FSD EU approval may face further delays
risks.description: Already pushed from February to 3/20. Regulatory uncertainty persists.

risks.title: Competition intensifying — direct sales monopoly eroding
risks.description: Washington state now allows Rivian/Lucid direct sales. Tesla's regulatory advantage weakening.

buyReasons.title: Double catalyst imminent: GTC + FSD EU approval
buyReasons.rationale: GTC keynote 3/16 + FSD EU approval 3/20 — two potential catalysts in one week. Success could trigger strong rebound.

buyReasons.title: European sales rebound signal
buyReasons.rationale: +10% YoY European registrations break 13-month decline. Possible bottom confirmation.

buyReasons.title: Energy business high growth continues
buyReasons.rationale: Energy revenue $12.8B (+27% YoY). Megapack demand explosion. Growth axis beyond vehicles.

buyReasons.title: Net cash $29.3B — healthy balance sheet
buyReasons.rationale: D/E 0.18, cash $44B. Overwhelming financial stability vs. competitors.

overallOpinion: Tesla at $391.20 has entered a technically weak zone, trading below both 50-day ($420.64) and 200-day ($393.45) moving averages.
Reuters' warning of a 'third consecutive year of delivery decline' and the ongoing executive exodus are weighing on fundamentals.
However, GTC keynote (3/16) and FSD EU approval (3/20) provide two catalysts concentrated in one week.
P/E of 387x is extreme, but energy/robotics/FSD narratives could justify re-rating if they materialize.
Short-term: Await catalyst outcomes. Medium-term: Need earnings turnaround confirmation. Hold stance appropriate.

sources.name: StockAnalysis
sources.description: TSLA real-time statistics, valuation, and financial data

sources.name: Reuters
sources.description: Third consecutive year delivery decline concern

threads.text: $391.20. Europe rebounded +10% but Reuters warns of '3rd straight delivery decline.' Next week: GTC + FSD EU approval — two catalysts in one week. Will they deliver? 🔗 investory.kro.kr/stock/TSLA/analysis/2026-03-14

telegram.hook: 🚗 Tesla $391 — Below Moving Averages, But Catalysts Await
telegram.text:
